signals.idea: Debug and fix all video editor bugs: (1) Player keeps re-rendering/stopping-starting - investigate if React 18 + Remotion causes constant re-renders, or if debounced save/React Query invalidation causes it; (2) Remove timeline list page - back button goes to home, auto-default into main timeline; (3) Fix render button (currently stub); (4) Fix drag-from-GenerationsPane into assets/timeline; (5) Fix text editing appearing small during edit; (6) Fix play button blocked by overlay/asset z-index; (7) General stability audit of the video editor

unresolved_flags.concern: Step 2.1 proposes `useMemo(() => data?.resolvedConfig ?? null, [data?.signature])` but `resolvedConfig` at line 471 is already a bare derivation (`data?.resolvedConfig ?? null`). The real problem is that `data` itself gets replaced by `commitData` on every poll/asset-registry sync — so `data?.resolvedConfig` is a new object each time `data` changes. The proposed fix is directionally correct but the code snippet is misleading: `data?.signature` changes whenever `data` changes (they're on the same object), so the memo would break if `data` reference changes but signature stays the same, which is the exact scenario. The memo needs to use a *separate* signature ref, not `data?.signature`.

Step 2 misses that `RemotionPreview.tsx:32-42` computes `metadata` (fps, durationInFrames, compositionWidth, compositionHeight) from `config` — and these are spread as individual props on the Remotion `<Player>`. Even if `inputProps` identity is stable, changes to `durationInFrames` (recalculated from config on every new config identity) will cause Remotion Player to remount. The `metadata` useMemo depends on `[config]` (line 42), so a new `config` identity = new metadata = Player remount regardless of inputProps stability.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 4.0. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 2.

warnings: Scope creep detected: the plan appears to be expanding beyond the original idea or recorded user notes.

items: Player plays continuously without stopping/restarting during idle editing and debounced saves
Navigating to /tools/video-editor auto-loads the most recent (or newly created) timeline without showing a list page
Back button in editor navigates to / (home)
Render button shows a clear 'not available yet' toast instead of setting a confusing error state
Dragging a generation from GenerationsPane to AssetPanel registers the asset; dragging to timeline creates a clip
Inline text editing textarea displays text at the same visual scale as the rendered composition
Play/pause controls are always clickable regardless of overlay positions
During playback, only PreviewPanel and OverlayEditor re-render per frame; TimelineEditor and PropertiesPanel remain stable

unresolved_flags.concern: Step 2.1 proposes `useMemo(() => data?.resolvedConfig ?? null, [data?.signature])` but `resolvedConfig` at line 471 is already a bare derivation (`data?.resolvedConfig ?? null`). The real problem is that `data` itself gets replaced by `commitData` on every poll/asset-registry sync — so `data?.resolvedConfig` is a new object each time `data` changes. The proposed fix is directionally correct but the code snippet is misleading: `data?.signature` changes whenever `data` changes (they're on the same object), so the memo would break if `data` reference changes but signature stays the same, which is the exact scenario. The memo needs to use a *separate* signature ref, not `data?.signature`.
unresolved_flags.evidence: Line 471: `const resolvedConfig = data?.resolvedConfig ?? null;` — `data` is state set by `commitData`. The proposed `useMemo(..., [data?.signature])` would still re-evaluate when `data` object reference changes since React tracks the dep value, not the object it came from. If `data` is swapped to a new object with the same `.signature` string, the memo dep value is the same string, so it would actually work. But this is fragile — it relies on string equality of `signature` across object replacements and doesn't account for the `metadata` useMemo at RemotionPreview.tsx:32-42 which also depends on `config` identity and will trigger a full Player re-render via changed `durationInFrames`/resolution props.

unresolved_flags.concern: Step 2 misses that `RemotionPreview.tsx:32-42` computes `metadata` (fps, durationInFrames, compositionWidth, compositionHeight) from `config` — and these are spread as individual props on the Remotion `<Player>`. Even if `inputProps` identity is stable, changes to `durationInFrames` (recalculated from config on every new config identity) will cause Remotion Player to remount. The `metadata` useMemo depends on `[config]` (line 42), so a new `config` identity = new metadata = Player remount regardless of inputProps stability.
unresolved_flags.evidence: RemotionPreview.tsx:32-42: `const metadata = useMemo(() => { ... }, [config]);` — fps, durationInFrames, compositionWidth, compositionHeight all passed directly to `<Player>`. Remotion Player remounts on prop changes to these values.
